findings.evidence: Lines 55-80 define `groupLikeCoact`, `groupLike`, `groupLike_coact_apply`, and `Hom.ofGroupLike` under only `[AddCommMonoid C] [Module R C] [Coalgebra R C]`, but each declaration is marked `private`; line 106 publicly exposes only `def trivial` under `[Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]`.
findings.fix: Expose the coalgebra-level API, e.g. public `Comodule.groupLike (g : GroupLike R C) : Comodule R C M`, public simp lemmas for `coact m = m ⊗ₜ[R] (g : C)`, and public `Hom.ofGroupLike`; then define `trivial` as the `g = 1` specialization.
findings.issue: `groupLike`, its coaction lemmas, and `Hom.ofGroupLike` are all `private`, so users only get the bialgebraic `trivial` specialization even though the construction needs only `[Coalgebra R C]` plus `g : GroupLike R C`.

summary: The implementation proves the natural coalgebra/group-like construction but hides it, leaving the public API at the bialgebra `1` specialization only. That is under-generalized for the stated comodule-over-coalgebra layer.
